scenario_results.reason: Standard-change guidance stayed narrow, evidence-based, and approval-path aware.
Missing rollback and validation evidence was surfaced before approval readiness was implied.
Emergency-change guardrails remained explicit while incident command was routed out of scope.
Business impact, freeze windows, and rollback risk were prioritized over requester urgency.
CAB-ready summary stayed focused on change-control quality and dependency visibility.
Calendar conflict and coordination risk were identified without drifting into execution planning.
Weak testing evidence was rejected and concrete validation expectations were stated.
Post-implementation review guidance was handled in scope while root-cause ownership was routed to problem management.
Direct implementation was rejected clearly with routing to technical specialists.
Broad governance ownership was rejected without widening the specialty boundary.
Low-confidence handling requested minimum missing evidence and avoided speculative approval guidance.
Fallback handling preserved the local reviewed baseline and avoided claims beyond specialist-owned evidence.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against issue #109 requirements, including normal change guidance, ticket and workflow quality, service-impact prioritization, edge-case handling, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior.
Acceptance thresholds exceed the issue minimum with 12 scenarios and a 1.00 pass rate against a 0.92 threshold.
Human verification is recorded in readiness/evidence.json and aligned to the validated delivery contract for issue #109.
Local repository validation was run with npm run lint, npm run verify:structure, and npm test.
